{{Quote|Huff Darklighter? The head of the Darklighter Estate? He's only the biggest business magnate on Tatooine.|Huff Darklighter's guard, to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:FHR">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Finding Huff's Rifle|Tatooine}}</ref>}}
'''Huff Darklighter''' was the [[Parent/Legends|father]] of [[Biggs Darklighter/Legends|Biggs Darklighter]], the brother of [[Jula Darklighter]], the uncle of [[Gavin Darklighter]], and a land baron on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]].

==Biography==
A native of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], Huff owned dozens of moisture farms on his home planet, and bought water from other farmers at cut-rate prices. Some of the water he used in his own hydroponics facilities, while most was sold to other settlers at a high profit. He used his ability to locate water to become one of the planet's largest food magnates. In turn, he used his wealth to make sure Biggs got everything he deserved from life.

Huff was not above bribing the local officials—both planetary and Imperial—to maintain his stature. Huff was avidly anti-Imperial, believing they had destroyed democracy and ruled through fear, and did not approve of Biggs' choice to enlist in the [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy]].

Prior to [[10 BBY/Legends|10 BBY]], Huff was widowed when [[Biggs Darklighter's mother/Legends|Biggs' mother]] died.<ref name="X-Wing: The Bacta War">''[[X-Wing: The Bacta War]]''</ref> Huff subsequently married [[Huff Darklighter's second wife|another woman]], with whom he had a daughter, [[Dera Darklighter]]<ref name="Insider65">{{InsiderCite|65|Who's Who in Star Wars Galaxies|Who's Who in ''Star Wars'' Galaxies}}</ref>  Huff would suffer another tragedy when Biggs was killed in action during the trench run at the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]<ref name="ANH">{{Film|IV}}</ref>

[[File:Huff_Darklighter.jpg|thumb|left|200px|[[Biggs Darklighter/Legends|Biggs]] confides in his father about his lack of confidence in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].]]
He held his son in highest regard, and truly believed that Biggs was the only hero of the Battle of Yavin. Huff went so far as to alter say that [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] was less than a hero for leaving the battle, despite the fact that Wedge was ordered out by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]].

Shaken by Biggs' death, Huff decided he wanted more heirs.  His wife, however, did not wish to have any more children than the one she'd already had, leading to the end of their marriage.  Huff subsequently married a third time, this time to [[Lanal Darklighter|Lanal]], the sister of Jula's wife [[Silya Darklighter|Silya]].  Huff and Lanal would have numerous children.<ref name="X-Wing: The Bacta War" />

After the Battle of Yavin, an antique [[Tusken King (rank)|Tusken King's]] [[Cycler rifle/Legends|rifle]] that belonged to Huff Darklighter's grandfather was stolen. Huff was very upset and one of his guards hired a spacer to retrieve the rifle. The spacer traveled to [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] and managed to recover the rifle from the [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Borvo]] the Hutt.<ref name="SWG:FHR" />

The spacer later worked for Huff Darklighter. For a first mission, Huff requested the spacer to help his daughter Dera who had become obsessed with [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]]. The spacer met the woman in the [[Lowland Basin/Legends|desert]] and agreed to eradicate a tribe of vile Sand Peoples dwelling within a [[Tusken Cave|cave]] near the Darklighter estate. For a second mission, Huff asked the spacer to disrupt [[Sennex]] [[Slavery/Legends|slave trade]] operations in [[Anchorhead/Legends|Anchorhead]]. The spacer thus attacked a [[Sennex Slave Bunker|Sennex bunker]] on the outskirts of the city that posed as a research facility and killed a number of slavers. For all the services provided to his family, Huff Darklighter rewarded a polished [[Krayt dragon/Legends|krayt dragon]] [[Skeleton/Legends|skull]] to the spacer.<ref name="SWG:DE">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Darklighter Estate|Tatooine}}</ref>

In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> one of [[Huff Darklighter's niece|Huff's nieces]] was kidnapped by [[Slavery/Legends|slavers]] working for [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]]. However, she quickly managed to escape and called her uncle for help with her [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]]. Huff Darklighter soon dispatched a [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] to rescue the woman before Jabba's henchmen tracking her showed up. The spacer found the woman lost in the desert and escorted her back safely to the Darklighter estate. Huff Darklighter later paid the spacer to eliminate the criminal leading Jabba's slaving ring.<ref name="SWG:HD">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=static|Huff Darklighter|Tatooine}}</ref>

Huff tried to obtain ownership of the [[Lars homestead/Legends|Lars moisture farm]] on Tatooine by initiating an alien landowner tax. The tax was geared toward breaking the alien [[Throgg]]'s financial reserves, but [[Gavin Darklighter|Gavin's]] father intervened and bought the farm before Huff could enforce the tax. Huff's dealings with the Imperial prefect on Tatooine allowed him a greater latitude in his dealings, and he worked hard to keep his competition at bay. However, Huff wasn't perfect. He was tricked and swindled by [[Lirin Banolt]], but managed to obtain an Imperial data disk containing information on the [[Eidolon Base]] weapons cache. The plans were stolen from his residence, but were eventually [[Tatooine Campaign|recovered]] by [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]].

Huff took control of much of the Eidolon weapons cache in the aftermath, and used some of the [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] armor, repainted to dark blue, for his security forces. Much of the remaining equipment was provided to Rogue Squadron during the [[Bacta War]]. Huff attempted to start a bidding war between the Rogues and an unknown buyer represented by [[Booster Terrik]], but upon learning that his long-time friend Wedge Antilles was seeking the equipment, Booster quickly switched his allegiance. He used his knowledge of Huff's Imperial dealings to blackmail the moisture farmer into giving the weapons to Wedge. When Huff attempted to haggle over the price, Booster informed him that he meant the weapons should be given away for free, but his daughter [[Mirax Terrik Horn|Mirax Terrik]] intervened, insisting that the Rogues would buy the weapons, but only at a fair price.<ref name="X-Wing: The Bacta War" />

==Behind the scenes==
In the [[Galaxy Guide 1: A New Hope|first edition]] of ''[[Galaxy Guide 1: A New Hope]]'', he was called "Huk Darklighter."

Huff Darklighter appeared as a [[Wikipedia:Non-player character|non-player character]] in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:HD" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> Huff Darklighter was present in the original release of the game, being the main quest giver of the [[W:c:swg:Darklighter's Estate questline (pre-NGE)|Darklighter's Estate questline]].<ref name="SWG:HD" /> With the release of the "[[W:c:swg:Publish 25 Notes for November 15, 2005|Publish 25]]," a.k.a. the "[[W:c:swg:New Game Enhancements|New Game Enhancements]]," on [[November 15]], [[2005]],<ref name="SonyCite-Publish Archive 2005">{{SonyCite|url=players/content.vm?id=64886&resource=publish|text=Publish Archive 2005}}</ref> the Darklighter's Estate questline was deeply [[W:c:swg:Darklighter's Estate questline|revamped]]. In addition to the overhaul of the entire storyline, Huff Darklighter had his appearance changed.<ref name="SWG:DE" />
